child_2m_5y.label: Sick child (2 months up to 5 years)

general_danger_signs.description: Check ALL sick children for general danger signs. Any single sign = urgent referral.

signs.label: Not able to drink or breastfeed
signs.how_to_check: Ask the mother: is the child able to drink or breastfeed? If not sure, ask her to offer the breast or a cup of water and observe.

signs.label: Vomits everything
signs.how_to_check: Ask: does the child vomit everything? A child who vomits everything cannot keep down any food, fluids, or medicines.

signs.label: Convulsions
signs.how_to_check: Ask: has the child had convulsions during this illness? Convulsions are uncontrolled stiffening and jerking movements.

signs.label: Lethargic or unconscious
signs.how_to_check: Check: is the child lethargic or unconscious? A lethargic child is not awake and alert when they should be. They may be drowsy and not respond normally to sounds or touch.

if_any_present.action: Complete assessment immediately. Give pre-referral treatment. REFER URGENTLY to hospital.

ask: Does the child have cough or difficult breathing?
look_listen_feel: Count the breaths in one minute (child must be calm)

classifications.name: SEVERE PNEUMONIA OR VERY SEVERE DISEASE
classifications.criteria: Any general danger sign OR chest indrawing OR stridor in calm child
treatment: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic
Give first dose of artesunate if high malaria risk

classifications.name: PNEUMONIA
treatment: Give appropriate oral antibiotic for 5 days

classifications.name: COUGH OR COLD
classifications.criteria: No signs of pneumonia or very severe disease
treatment: Soothe throat and cough with safe remedy
Follow up in 5 days if not improving

ask: Is there blood in the stool?
look_feel: Look at the child's general condition (lethargic/unconscious, restless/irritable)
Offer fluid: is the child not able to drink, drinking eagerly, or drinking normally?
Pinch the skin of the abdomen: does it go back very slowly, slowly, or immediately?

dehydration_classifications.name: SEVERE DEHYDRATION
dehydration_classifications.criteria: Two or more: lethargic/unconscious, sunken eyes, not able to drink, skin pinch very slow
treatment: If other severe classification: refer urgently with ORS

dehydration_classifications.name: SOME DEHYDRATION
dehydration_classifications.criteria: Two or more: restless/irritable, sunken eyes, drinks eagerly, skin pinch slow
treatment: Give ORS (Plan B): 4 hours in facility
Follow up in 5 days if not improving

dehydration_classifications.name: NO DEHYDRATION
dehydration_classifications.criteria: Not enough signs for some or severe dehydration
treatment: Give ORS to take home (Plan A)

ask: Does the child have fever (by history or feels hot or temperature 37.5C or above)?
Has the child had measles within the last 3 months?
look_feel: Look or feel for stiff neck
Look for signs of measles (generalized rash, cough/runny nose/red eyes)
Do malaria RDT if in malaria risk area

classifications.name: VERY SEVERE FEBRILE DISEASE
classifications.criteria: Any general danger sign OR stiff neck
treatment: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic
Treat to prevent low blood sugar

classifications.name: FEVER - MALARIA UNLIKELY
classifications.criteria: RDT negative or no malaria risk, no danger signs, no stiff neck
treatment: Follow up in 2 days if fever persists
